items.mistake: Treating SVM decision_function output as probability
items.fix: Use decision scores for ranking and ROC/AUC; apply Platt scaling or isotonic calibration if calibrated probabilities are required.
items.why: f(x) = wᵀx + b is a margin score, not P(y=1|x). Threshold 0 on scores is not the same as threshold 0.5 on sigmoid output.
items.example: Wrong: proba = clf.decision_function(X); treat 0.3 as 30% chance. Right: rank by score for AUC; use CalibratedClassifierCV if probabilities needed.

items.mistake: Training SVM on unscaled features with mixed units
items.fix: Standardize or normalize continuous features before SVC/LinearSVC so C penalizes dimensions fairly.
items.why: Margin geometry uses L2 norm; one feature in dollars vs another in years distorts ||w|| and effective margin.
items.example: Apply StandardScaler in a pipeline before LinearSVC(C=1.0) when income and age are both inputs.

items.mistake: Using hard-margin SVM on noisy real-world data
items.fix: Use soft-margin with tuned C (and kernels if needed). Expect support vectors among mislabeled or borderline points.
items.why: Hard margin requires perfect linear separability — one outlier can make the problem infeasible.
items.example: SVC(kernel='linear', C=1.0) on overlapping classes instead of C=np.inf hard-margin attempt that fails to converge.

items.mistake: Assuming all training points are support vectors
items.fix: Inspect clf.support_ or n_support_ — typically only a fraction of points constrain the boundary.
items.why: Points far beyond the margin have zero dual weight and do not affect the solution.
items.example: 10k training rows but len(clf.support_) == 400 — only 400 define the boundary.
